authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Inventory Tool
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS quality-measure inventory is authoritative for CMS measure identification, stewarding, measure programs, and measure specification references used to validate numerator, denominator, exclusion, and reporting context.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality metric definition and source-specification intake

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Measures Management System Blueprint
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS measure-management guidance is authoritative for measure lifecycle, specification, testing, implementation, maintenance, and evidence expectations used to constrain healthcare metric claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Measure lifecycle, specification, and maintenance control

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official HHS HIPAA guidance is authoritative for limiting protected health information in healthcare analytics outputs, examples, disclosure packets, and escalations to the minimum necessary for the stated purpose.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, audience review, and disclosure minimization

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS claims-processing manuals are authoritative for Medicare claim workflow, provider billing record context, claim-state semantics, and payer/provider operational evidence used in encounter-to-claim reconciliation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Encounter, charge, claim, remittance, and denial validation

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS prior-authorization guidance is authoritative for prior-authorization process, documentation, payer response, and patient-access workflow dependencies that can gate analytics interpretation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, referral, authorization, and payer-dependency checks

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability
authoritative_sources.publisher: Office of the National Coordinator for Health Information Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official ONC interoperability baseline is authoritative for healthcare data classes and terminology used to trace patient, encounter, problem, procedure, medication, laboratory, and clinical-operational evidence in analytics.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Healthcare data lineage and terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / eCQI Resource Center
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official federal eCQI resource is authoritative for eCQM specifications, QRDA implementation guidance, value sets, and standards-based quality-measure evidence handling.

authoritative_sources.title: HEDIS Measures and Technical Resources
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NCQA is the steward of HEDIS; its public measure and technical-resource guidance is authoritative for health-plan quality measure terminology, denominator, exclusion, and reporting workflow expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Health-plan quality measure and benchmark handling

authoritative_sources.title: Provider Data Catalog and Care Compare
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official CMS public provider-data platform is authoritative for public-reporting posture, provider-data display context, dataset lineage, and external disclosure caution when internal metrics are compared with public healthcare performance data.

workflow_stages.name: Tenant, audience, patient/member, and reporting-context intake
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, business unit, program, product, payer, plan, facility, provider type, service setting, reporting period, patient/member status, intended audience, disclosure posture, and source-precedence rules before analysis starts.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization dependency validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, referral, authorization, service date, coverage, consent, and documentation dependencies when they affect access, utilization, claim, denial, or operational metrics.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Metric definition and measure logic control
workflow_stages.description: Define numerator, denominator, exclusions, attribution, cohort, refresh window, source tables, measure references, and publication thresholds before calculation or narrative synthesis.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Source collection, reconciliation, and confidence scoring
workflow_stages.description: Collect and reconcile scheduling, EHR, EDW, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, denial, quality, utilization-review, and BI records while preserving source conflicts and lag assumptions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Analysis package assembly and caveat drafting
workflow_stages.description: Assemble metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveats, confidence limits, source-lineage notes, exception registers, and recommended next questions for the approved audience and cadence.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Privacy, disclosure, and adjacent-authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Stop when PHI handling, minimum-necessary posture, public reporting, clinical judgment, patient safety, payer interpretation, coding policy, legal interpretation, quality attestation, or operational approval is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: tenant policy, jurisdiction, business unit, program, product, payer, plan, provider type, facility type, service setting, and reporting-audience records
patient or member identity, eligibility, status, service dates, consent, referral, authorization, and documentation dependency records
encounter, scheduling, referral, authorization, charge, claim, remittance, denial, and utilization-review extracts
EHR reporting, enterprise data warehouse, BI semantic model, revenue-cycle reporting, quality reporting, and scheduling analytics outputs
quality measure specifications, numerator and denominator logic, exclusions, value sets, eCQM, QRDA, HEDIS, benchmark, and public-reporting references
data-quality exception logs, source-reconciliation notes, metric dictionaries, refresh logs, lineage maps, disclosure-risk notes, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: enterprise data warehouse and governed BI semantic layer
EHR reporting and clinical documentation system
scheduling, access, referral, and authorization platforms
revenue-cycle, billing, clearinghouse, claims, remittance, denial, and payment posting systems
quality reporting, eCQM, HEDIS, registry, utilization review, and public-reporting platforms
payer, plan, eligibility, coverage, and authorization data stores
privacy, consent, disclosure, compliance, and approval workflow systems

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing healthcare prerequisites
choose the authoritative metric definition, source hierarchy, denominator, numerator, exclusions, source tables, refresh window, and cadence for descriptive analytics
determine whether data quality, denominator logic, lineage, and confidence are good enough to publish an internal analytical result
prepare metric specifications, analytical summaries, caveat notes, data-quality exception registers, reconciliation notes, and next-evidence request lists
escalate: required tenant facts, patient/member status, payer, plan, service setting, source hierarchy, eligibility, referral, authorization, consent, or retrieval hooks are missing and would change the answer
public guidance, payer policy, tenant policy, measure specifications, or system records materially conflict
PHI handling, privacy obligation, minimum-necessary analysis, low-volume disclosure, patient/member safety, clinical risk, or public-reporting posture changes the answer
clinical, payer-policy, coding, medical-necessity, legal, compliance, quality attestation, revenue-cycle approval, or operational leadership authority is required
refuse: publish or imply certainty when source quality, signoff, definitions, denominator logic, or disclosure authority is unresolved
diagnose, plan treatment, determine medical necessity, make clinical judgments, or substitute analytics for licensed review
reinterpret payer policy, coverage rules, coding policy, appeal strategy, privacy law, or regulatory obligations
state unsupported causal, public-facing, regulatory, benchmark, staffing, safety, or policy conclusions
disclose PHI or identifiable examples without minimum-necessary and approval context

domain_failure_modes: treating healthcare analytics as generic BI and failing to request payer, plan, facility, provider type, service setting, patient/member status, source hierarchy, or privacy context
collapsing scheduling, encounter, charge, claim, remittance, denial, referral, authorization, quality, and utilization-review records into one truth set without explaining lags and ownership
publishing quality or utilization metrics without explicit numerator, denominator, exclusions, measure source, cohort logic, refresh window, and public-reporting posture
inferring eligibility, referral, authorization, payer coverage, medical necessity, or denial appeal outcomes from incomplete administrative data
using PHI, low-volume cohorts, or patient/member examples without minimum-necessary, consent, audience, and approval controls
turning descriptive trends into unsupported causal, clinical, patient-safety, payer-policy, compliance, or staffing conclusions
letting the analytics lane widen into licensed clinical judgment, privacy-law interpretation, coding policy, quality attestation, regulatory submission, or public disclosure approval

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A stakeholder asks for a metric with ambiguous inclusion or exclusion rules.
Claims and encounter data disagree on the same operational outcome.
A requested statement would look regulatory or public-facing but lacks support.
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes.
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist.
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing.
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis.
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific metric dictionaries and payer-specific operating rules
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Healthcare tenants differ on access, utilization, denial, leakage, and quality KPI definitions by payer, product, service setting, facility type, and contract. The package treats these as runtime prerequisites and requires public or tenant baseline plus explicit tenant override before publishing.

State law, payer contracts, accreditation programs, and tenant policy can be stricter than federal public guidance. The package escalates when local privacy, disclosure, clinical-risk, or reporting authority changes the answer.

notes: Authoritative healthcare analytics records are the tenant-designated EHR, EDW, BI, scheduling, referral, authorization, claim, remittance, denial, utilization-review, quality-reporting, payer, and disclosure systems constrained by the cited public guidance.

The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, coding-policy interpretation, privacy-law interpretation, quality attestation, public disclosure approval, regulatory submission, and patient-safety judgment.
